title: cuscuta chinensis var. ciliaris
intro: Cuscuta chinensis var. ciliaris, also known as Chinese dodder, is a parasitic plant that grows by attaching itself to other plants and obtaining nutrients through a network of thin stems. It has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Cuscuta chinensis var. ciliaris are numerous, including its ability to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and enhance immune function. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is also used in combination with other herbs to create medicinal formulations.
